issue_title: Inline python declared with `exec s:inline_python` results in treating python as error
issue_body:
While the following vimscript is linted correctly:
```VimL
python << EOF
import os
EOF
```
the following, even though equivalen, results in an error on third line:
```VimL
let s:inline_python = 'python << EOF'
exec s:inline_python
import os
EOF
```
This way of declaring inline python can be useful when `s:inline_python` has different value depending on the python interpreter that should be used.

issue_title: Fake ISBN10 causes "Registrant/Publication not found"
issue_body:
In rare cases the `fake.isbn10` method throws an exception with the following message: `Exception: Registrant/Publication not found in registrant rule list. `

The full 
### Steps to reproduce

Call `faker.providers.isbn.Provider._registrant_publication` with any of the following values for the `reg_pub` param: `64799998`, `39999999`. These values are valid randomly generated strings from [L34](https://github.com/joke2k/faker/blob/master/faker/providers/isbn/__init__.py#L37).

Code:
```python
from faker.providers.isbn import Provider
from faker.providers.isbn.rules import RULES

# Fails; throws an exception
Provider._registrant_publication('64799998', RULES['978']['0'])
Provider._registrant_publication('39999999', RULES['978']['1'])

# Works; but may be invalid
Provider._registrant_publication('64799998', RULES['978']['1'])
Provider._registrant_publication('39999999', RULES['978']['0'])
```

### Expected behavior

The `faker.providers.isbn.Provider._body` should generate valid `reg_pub` values.

### Actual behavior

It generates values for `reg_pub` that are not accepted by the rules defined in `faker.providers.isbn.rules`.

issue_title: import IP protocols can import modules from other packages and fail.
issue_body:
_From [de...@chromium.org](https://code.google.com/u/117933008161009266207/) on October 10, 2013 14:30:25_

If you have installed a python module somewhere on your path that has the same name as a protocol listed on the ip.py module (like IP_PROTO_ROUTING attempts to import the "routing" module) your module could be imported in dpkt instead of the intended one, and latter fail because protocol.PROTOCOL is expected.

To fix this, we need to pass levels=0 to the __import__ on ip.py. Attached is the patch to fix this with an extra check.

Thanks :)

**Attachment:** [ip-import-fix.patch](http://code.google.com/p/dpkt/issues/detail?id=115)

_Original issue: http://code.google.com/p/dpkt/issues/detail?id=115_

issue_title: Mapping cDNA reads
issue_body:
**Update:** the "cdna" branch has been merged into "master" and subsequently deleted.

<hr>

**Original post:**

This is a feature request from twitter users. A *very preliminary* implementation is available at the [cdna branch](https://github.com/lh3/minimap2/tree/cdna). Some notes:

* perform global alignment (not local hits to each exon)
* introns reported as long deletions (which made possible by the 2-piece gap penalty)
* allow up to 100kb intron by default (longer threshold reduces performance)
* deletions longer than 68bp are considered as introns
* find small exons if nearby exons are long
* splicing boundaries are not accurate
* the cDNA mode is slower due to more DP-based alignment

To use it,
```sh
git clone https://github.com/lh3/minimap2
git checkout -b cdna remotes/origin/cdna
make
./minimap2 -a -x cdna ref.fa reads.fa
./minimap2 -a -x cdna -k14 -G 50000 ref.fa reads.fa # parameter tuning
```

`cdna` is an experimental branch. If it is not totally crap, I will refine it and merge it to the master. Your feedbacks are warmly welcomed and greatly appreciated. I don't have a good sense about what is a good cDNA alignment for now.

issue_title: Rationale behind the "_get_name_from_args" function in parameter.py
issue_body:
I don't really understand why this function `_get_name_from_args` is needed. It looks like it processes args and keyword args, looking for specific arguments, and returns them, but this is exactly what Python already does for us. For instance, I don't see why the two following cases are different:

```python
class Uniform(Base):
    """
    A specialization of the Base parameter for uniform distributions
    """

    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        name, low, high, args, kwargs = self._get_name_from_args('low high', *args, **kwargs)
        super(Uniform, self).__init__(name, rnd.uniform, (low, high), *args, **kwargs)
```

and

```python
class Uniform(Base):
    """
    A specialization of the Base parameter for uniform distributions
    """

    def __init__(self, name, low, high, *args, **kwargs):
        super(Uniform, self).__init__(name, rnd.uniform, (low, high), *args, **kwargs)
```

The latter looks easier to understand and makes the arguments the `Uniform` constructor needs very explicit.

Unless there is a specific usage (such as dynamically deciding the distribution and using that, though I couldn't find anything like that in the codebase), I don't see why it should be written like this.

issue_title: Getting characteristics for empty services throws exception
issue_body:
bluepy seems to be unable to cope with peripherals which have empty services (i.e. they have no characteristics).

If you get services characteristics by calling the 'getCharacteristics()' method on an instance of a 'Service' object and that service has no characteristics, the exception "bluepy.btle.BTLEException: Error from Bluetooth stack (comerr)" is thrown.

"What is the point of services with no characteristics?", you may well ask but it seems that the BLE specification mandates the Generic Attribute service (0x1801) which includes only a single characteristic which is *optional*. So, it would seem that peripherals that have empty services might actually be quite common. Indeed I experienced this issue while trying to connect to Google's Daydream VR controller.

issue_title: Python3 compatibility
issue_body:
Apparently in python 3 the print statement was replaced with a print function, meaning that instead of 
```print x``` you need to call ```print(x)```. Consequently, this breaks mir_eval on Python 3 (brought to my attention by someone trying to use mir_eval on Windows 7 with Python 3.4). 

Perhaps we should replace all calls to ```print x``` with e.g. ```sys.stdout.write(x)``` which should work both on python 2 and 3?

issue_title: serpy no longer supports None as field values
issue_body:
As a result of #38 serpy the following things happen when `None` is returned from a `MethodField`
* If `required=True` (default), serialization throws an exception
* If `required=False`, the key and value are not included in the output dictionary.

I'm using MethodField because it's a concrete example. I believe the same thing happens with most (if not all) other fields.

As you might expect, this breaks any use case of serpy that needs to return None.

The [django rest framework docs](http://www.django-rest-framework.org/api-guide/fields/#required) state
> Setting this to False also allows the object attribute or dictionary key to be omitted from output when serializing the instance. If the key is not present it will simply not be included in the output representation.

In concrete terms, the following serializer returns the data as seen below

```python
class DrfSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
    value = serializers.IntegerField(required=...)
```

| DRF 3.7.3 | `required=True` | `required=False` |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `data = {'value': 1}` |  `{'value': 1}` |  `{'value': 1}` |
| `data = {'value': None}` |  `{'value': None}` |  `{'value': None}` |
| `data = {}` |  `KeyError(u"Got KeyError when...` |  `{}` |

When using the following serpy 0.2.0 serializer I get the results below

```python
class SerpySerializer(serpy.DictSerializer):
    value = serpy.Field(required=...)
```

| serpy 0.2.0 | `required=True` | `required=False` |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `data = {'value': 1}` |  `{'value': 1}` |  `{'value': 1}` |
| `data = {'value': None}` |  `TypeError('Field {0} is required', 'value')` |  `{}` |
| `data = {}` |  `KeyError('value',)` |  `KeyError('value',)` |

For reference, this is the output from serpy 0.1.1

| serpy 0.1.1 | `required=True` | `required=False` |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `data = {'value': 1}` |  `{'value': 1}` |  `{'value': 1}` |
| `data = {'value': None}` |  `{'value': None}` |  `{'value': None}` |
| `data = {}` |  `KeyError('value',)` |  `KeyError('value',)` |

As seen in the tables, PR #38 does the following things
* breaks cases that previously matched DRF behavior
* adds no new cases that match DRF behavior
* breaks any serializers that wanted to return `None`.

For these reasons I'd like to suggest #38 be reverted.

  File "/tmp/easy_install-7pdx2q5r/srptools-0.1.1/srptools/context.py", line 4, in <module>
ImportError: No module named 'six'
```
The problem itself is quite simple. Since you are importing VERSION from srptools in setup.py, that will basically evaluate everything in that module. So context will be pulled in which in turn requires "six". Since this package is not part of setup_requires, it will not yet have been installed at this stage. A suggestion to work-around this is to place version in a separate file (that does not depend on anything) and just pull that one in. I have done a similar thing in my library:
https://github.com/postlund/pyatv/blob/master/setup.py
https://github.com/postlund/pyatv/blob/master/pyatv/const.py
I assume adding six to setup_requires might work as well, but I have not tried that myself. Is this anything that can be fixed soon? It's a bit of a usage blocker.

issue_title: Issue with Teradata SHOW SELECT * FROM SYNTAX
issue_body:
# Background
Teradata SHOW SELECT * FROM syntax gives details of all Tables and Views Underlying the view which is being queried.  
  
For Example -  
If View Definition is  
```sql
CREATE VIEW ABC 
AS SELECT a.a, b.b, c.c
FROM a
INNER JOIN b
ON a.col = b.col
INNER JOIN c
ON b.col1 = c.col1;
```  
The output of 
```sql
SHOW SELECT * FROM ABC
```
would give definition of View ABC, Table a, Table b and table c.

# Problem Description
when querying SHOW SELECT * FROM ABC, the output does not have all definitions but it contains only 1 table definition whichever shows first. And does not return the definition of other tables and full view.

issue_title: Couchapp support
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm working on couchapp (design documents) support for this module. It's inspired by erica (https://github.com/benoitc/erica).

For now it's in *pycouchdb.app* module and CLI tool has signature like this:

```
$ python -m pycouchdb.app push -h
usage: python -m pycouchdb.app push [-h] [--url COUCHDB_URL] [--db DBNAME]
                                    [--create-db]
                                    [APPDIRS [APPDIRS ...]]

positional arguments:
  APPDIRS            List of directories holding your couchapps. Defaults to
                     all directories found in your current working directory.

optional arguments:
  -h, --help         show this help message and exit
  --url COUCHDB_URL  CouchDB url. Defaults to `http://localhost:5984/`. For
                     basic auth, use e.g.
                     `http://user:password@localhost:5984`.
  --db DBNAME        Database name. Database must exist and you must have
                     sufficient permissions to read/write design documents
                     from/to it. If omitted, name of parent directory will be
                     used.
  --create-db        Create database if it does not exist.
```

In the end it should have support for operations:

* push - push directory as couchapp
* clone - clone existing couchapp
* create - create couchapp from included template

Would you like this to be part of pycouchdb? If so, do you have any suggestions?

Thank you!

issue_title: Show track details
issue_body: Show all metadata provided by Mopidy for a track. Probably via track context menu (add this also to tracklist), but also in playback view.

issue_title: Time interval is not behaving correctly
issue_body:
I'm an idiot. In #1225, @ultrabug asked me to open an issue so I opened an issue https://github.com/ultrabug/py3status/issues/1235 about that. I later realize he wanted me to open an issue about the time.

Copypasta.

>I switched to `self.py3.time_in(0)` here to keep it simple.
>
>I'm not sure if I mentioned this before... I think the timing is off by a second as the correct code should be `self.py3.time_in(1)` to update every `1` second. You can try this PR with `1` and will get `pomodoro` to count down every `2` seconds instead.
>
>Maybe we should hold this off to look into this as everything is now off by a second+.

I assume this is reasonable.
```
self.py3.time_in(-1)   # update once, stop forever
self.py3.time_in(0)    # update twice every second
self.py3.time_in(1)    # update every 1 second
self.py3.time_in(5)    # update every 5 second
```
This is noticeable in audio-playing modules using timed placeholders too.

issue_title: False-positive isinstance assert after generation
issue_body:
Python version: 3.6.5
OS: macOS 10.13.5
Tested versions: 0.1.0 and 0.1.1rc2

The following [line](https://github.com/vmagamedov/grpclib/blob/master/grpclib/encoding/proto.py#L8) gives false-positive AssertionError for generated class. When I removed the assert I was able to call the server and received the proper response.

How to reproduce:
To reproduce the issue the `helloworld_pb2.py` and `helloworld_grpc.py` files from hello world example should be re-generated with `grpc_tools` command inside the current directory.

- Go to directory `grpclib/example/helloworld/`
- Run the command

      python3 -m grpc_tools.protoc -I. --python_out=. --python_grpc_out=. helloworld.proto

be aware that it is called from the same directory where `.proto` file is located

- Run `server.py`
- Call server with command

      grpcurl -plaintext -proto helloworld.proto 127.0.0.1:50051 helloworld.Greeter/UnaryUnaryGreeting

This issue prevents you to store `.proto` files aside `.py` sources.

issue_title: Use templating to generate code files
issue_body:
Based on past experience I have found that generating source code files using templates is far more understandable and sustainable than manually managing structures and indenting as is done currently in the ``plugin/main.py``. 

I have modified the grplib package to use this approach, by using *jinja2*. If you are interested in seeing this let me know and I'll submit a pull request.

issue_title: gs issues
issue_body:
parallelism doesn't work. probably need to bring back copy_reg stuff. we didn't catch this because our test suite runs single-threaded, because nested parallelism fails. 

the user has to pass in `model__` for each param name for gs_params. make this easier by checking for model__ and, updating the param name accordingly.

issue_title: Set AppUserModelID in subprocesses when "--app-user-model-id" switch is provided (pinning to taskbar issues on Windows 10)
issue_body:
On Windows 10 there are issues with pinning app to taskbar, sometimes duplicate app icons appear on taskbar or sometimes updating taskbar icon doesn't work. On Windows 10 applications are required to set AppUserModelID (AppID) string that uniquely identifies application on taskbar and allows to group app's seperate windows under a single taskbar button [1].  If application uses multiple processes it should set AppID for each of these processes (especially UI processes that create windows). A win32 function for that is `SetCurrentProcessExplicitAppUserModelID` [2]. 

A Python application can make such call in the Python/Browser process using such code:
```py
import sys
if "win32" in sys.platform:
    import ctypes
    from ctypes.wintypes import HRESULT
    PCWSTR = ctypes.c_wchar_p
    AppUserModelID = ctypes.windll.shell32.SetCurrentProcessExplicitAppUserModelID
    AppUserModelID.argtypes = [PCWSTR]
    AppUserModelID.restype = HRESULT
    # An identifier that is globally unique for all apps running on Windows
    app_id = "change_this_to_some_unique_id"
    hresult = AppUserModelID(app_id)
    assert hresult == 0, "SetCurrentProcessExplicitAppUserModelID failed"
```

But that seems not enough. MSDN documentation [1] states that all app processes should set AppID. It seems that it is necessary to set it also for the Browser and Renderer processes in Chromium, as both of these processes create win32 windows. These two pull requests in the Electron and Atom project confirm that [3] [4].

The solution would be to pass the `--app-user-model-id` flag as a switch to `cef.Initialize()`. First this flag would need to be propagated from the Browser process to other processes and I think that passing switch to `cef.Initialize()` is enough to do that. Then the subprocess C++ code would need to check if this flag was passed and call `SetCurrentProcessExplicitAppUserModelID` [2] in that case, probably somewhere in `src/subprocess/cefpython_app.cpp`.

It is worth noting, even though it is a bit off-topic here, that it is also required to set AppUserModelID (AppID) property for app shortcuts (Desktop shortcut, Quick launch shortcut, Start > Programs shortcut) that are created by application installer.  For example in Inno Setup this can be specified using the `AppUserModelID` option in the `[Icons]` section [5]. Googling for "9F4C2855-9F79-4B39-A8D0-E1D42DE1D5F3" [6] returns results from Microsoft documentation related to setting AppUserModelID for app shortcuts. That GUID was taken from Squirrel code [7]. Here you can find registry file that enables additional information about app shortcut when clicking properties on it and that includes displaying AppUserModelID property [8].

Ref:
[1] https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/dd378459(v=vs.85).aspx
[2] https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/windows/desktop/dd378422(v=vs.85).aspx
[3] https://github.com/electron/electron/pull/2175
[4] https://github.com/atom/atom/pull/8170
[5] http://www.jrsoftware.org/ishelp/topic_iconssection.htm
[6] https://www.google.com/search?q=%229F4C2855-9F79-4B39-A8D0-E1D42DE1D5F3%22&hl=en&gfe_rd=cr&gws_rd=cr
[7] https://github.com/Squirrel/Squirrel.Windows/blob/8877944cf21a58c18341d6640bed2014582d1380/src/Squirrel/ShellFile.cs#L83
[8] http://winaero.com/blog/how-to-show-more-details-for-shortcuts-in-windows-8-1-windows-8-and-windows-7/

issue_title: trouble capturing stdout in test
issue_body:
I'm attempting to write some tests around telemetry of a flask app using pytest asserting on the contents of stdout. Suppose I have this app,

```
imports ...

app = Flask(__name__)

@app.route("/")
def hello():
    return "hello"

@app.before_first_request
def app_start():
    app.logger.addHandler(logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout))
    app.logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)

@app.after_request
def after_request(response):
   app.logger.info('signal')
```

and this test

```
imports ...

__flask__ = app.test_client()
__flask__.testing = True


def test_endpoint_produces_signal(capsys):
    __flask__.get('/')
    out, err = capsys.readouterr()
    assert out == 'signal'

```

Unfortunately this test fails. However, if I move the logging configuration and info call all to after_request is works. Thanks for your help.

The dictionary looks like this: {'comment': {'comment': 'Invalid TCP SYN packet'}, 'target': 'DROP', 'protocol': 'tcp', 'conntrack': {'ctstate': 'NEW'}, 'tcp': {'tcp-flags': ['!', 'FIN,SYN,RST,ACK', 'SYN']}}}

@ldx  Would this be something worth adding ? And if so, in what file should it be added?
Currently I have all these functions on a separate file, a helper, where I  import iptc...

Thanks!

issue_title: Installation Fails Because of __version__ Import (v0.6)
issue_body:
c88b376 tried to make things easier to manage by doing `from flask_user import __version__ as flask_user_version` in `setup.py`. However, that has an undesirable consequence: users who don't already have `passlib` installed could face an `ImportError`, preventing installation of Flask-User.

To reproduce (ensure `passlib` is not already installed):
```
$ pip install --no-cache-dir Flask-User==0.6.17
Collecting Flask-User==0.6.17 (from -r requirements.txt (line 13))
  Downloading Flask-User-0.6.17.tar.gz (62kB)
    100% |████████████████████████████████| 71kB 6.0MB/s
    Complete output from command python setup.py egg_info:
    Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
      File "/tmp/pip-build-c6f9o6kh/Flask-User/setup.py", line 5, in <module>
        from flask_user import __version__ as flask_user_version
      File "/tmp/pip-build-c6f9o6kh/Flask-User/flask_user/__init__.py", line 7, in <module>
        from passlib.context import CryptContext
    ImportError: No module named 'passlib'

    ----------------------------------------
Command "python setup.py egg_info" failed with error code 1 in /tmp/pip-build-c6f9o6kh/Flask-User/
```
Note that if the packages are cached, it could also appear to work fine, which is why `--no-cache-dir` is needed.

issue_title: @roles_required does not apply if placed before @app.route
Inline elements don't parse across newlines
issue_body:
When parsing Markdown, mistletoe doesn't parse inlines across line boundaries. Example source: ```markdown here is a file with *emph* and also it will have *emph across lines.* uh-oh. Let's try **strong** and also, of course, **strong across lines.** uh-oh-uh-oh. ``` And example output: ```html

here is a file with emph and also it will have *emph across lines.* uh-oh. Let's try strong and also, of course, **strong across lines.** uh-oh-uh-oh.

``` Most parsers recognize inlines over newlines. It'd be nice if mistletoe did, too. Would it be as simple as setting a `re` flag?

issue_title: Idea: web interface
issue_body:
Wondering what the general feeling is about something like a web interface for managing processes run by Honcho, for local development environments. Couple of things that could make it useful:

 * show at a glance which processes are running
 * one-click start / stop / restart for processes
 * filter logs on the front-end by string / regex
 * filter logs on the front-end by which process wrote the logs
 * click through to web processes

Is this an obviously bad idea for a reason I don't see? Is there already something like this? Should this be developed as part of `honcho`, or a stand-alone tool / wrapper?

issue_title: Integration with codecov.io
issue_body:
See codecov/support#364, in short coverxygen can be used to get doxygen coverage numbers on codecov.io by simply adding something like:
```
make doxygen
python -m coverxygen --xml-dir xml/ --src-dir . --output doxygen.coverage.info
bash <(curl -s https://codecov.io/bash) -R . -F doxygen -X gcov -f doxygen.coverage.info
```

issue_title: Allow running as root
issue_body:
There are obvious reasons why running as root would not normally be a good idea. However there are certain circumstances when it's perfectly ok. For example applications inside docker containers usually run as root because they should have full permissions inside their container. This is completely safe.

Having to create new users and groups unnecessarily complicates the Dockerfile and can cause permission issues when mounting volumes.

Would you be open to the idea of allowing root but just showing a warning? Or alternatively keeping the current exit on root behaviour but allow enabling root it with `ALLOW_ROOT=1` or similar?

If you're ok with this I'm happy to put a PR together. Great work btw!

issue_title: Validation of a dictionary runs in O(n^2)
issue_body:
Currently all keys in the dictionary are matched against all keys in the schema. If we have a large set of keys then the validation takes very long.

Here is a test that simulates the problem. A single validation should take under 1 ms but currently takes 12 ms.

```
def test_validation_performance():
    """
    Naive validation of a dictionary with many keys with a schema that has many keys
     This test verifies a single validation runs in under 1 msec
    :return:
    """
    number_if_keys_in_schema = 100
    iterations = 1000
    threshold_ms = 1

    schema_dict = {str(i): str for i in range(number_if_keys_in_schema)}

    schema = Schema(schema_dict)

    data = {str(i): str(i) for i in range(number_if_keys_in_schema)}

    start = time.time()

    for i in xrange(iterations):
        schema(data)

    total_elapsed = time.time() - start
    avg_per_round = round(total_elapsed * 1000 / iterations, 3)
    print("Total: %s secs, avg: %s ms" % (round(total_elapsed, 3), avg_per_round))

    assert avg_per_round < threshold_ms, "Avg validation %s > %s ms" % (avg_per_round, threshold_ms)
```

In recent versions of gunicorn however, the default method of deployment is to run `gunicorn` directly from command line rather than through django management.

So is there a way to somehow generate `bin/gunicorn` as the old method is now deprecated?

Note: the documentation does indeed mention `wsgi-script
    The name of the wsgi-script that is generated. This can be useful for gunicorn.` but doesn't say how.

issue_title: Rename __dataclass_post_init__ to just __post_init__.
issue_body:
I agree with @ilevkivskyi that we should use a shorter name, and leverage the fact that this is a stdlib object to lay claim to `__post_init__`. The longer name is annoying because users of the class have to implement a function with this name.

But I'd like to leave `__dataclass_fields__`, since that's truly an implementation detail and should never be used outside of dataclasses.py.

issue_title: stSort and stPersist not working if stSort contains expression + Solution
issue_body:
Hi,
sorry if this was already posted here. There are so many posts similar to my problem that I didn't know what to do. However, I also give a solution.

I found out that stSort does not correctly read the table state if the sort attribute is a method instead of property. I'm using stPersist to jsonfy the tablestate.

e.g.

``` javascript
<th st-sort="sort.aMethod"..>
```

where sort is a scope object with methods for sorting.

https://github.com/lorenzofox3/Smart-Table/blob/master/dist/smart-table.js
In Line 373 (in stSort) there is 

``` javascript
if (newValue.predicate !== predicate) {
```

where

``` javascript
var predicate = attr.stSort; 
```

in our example it is "sort.amethod".

However, if methods are used for sorting, the ctrl.tablestate().sort.predicate contains a function binding and not a string. So the if decision in L373 cannot work since nowhere in code the function tableState.sort.predicate is created and JSON.parse does not work with functions, of course. 
(As a side note: the functionName attribute, if a binding is a function, will be empty if the sorter object looks like this:

``` javascript
$scope.sorter = {
   aMethod : function () { return ... };
}
```

functionName uses prototype Function.name which will use the name between "function" and "()". However it is empty in most examples I've seen. On the other hand "name" of Function prototype doesn't seem to be readily available everywhere since it is a ES6 standard. See:
https://developer.mozilla.org/de/docs/Web/JavaScript/Reference/Global_Objects/Function/name
)

**SOLUTION**
I have created a plunk to show the problems
http://plnkr.co/edit/kqyv4I?p=preview

In the header you can find stSort with direct property name (PropertyPersistSinceName) of a row and also a method name of a sorter object in scope (EvalNotPersist). 
Click on EvalNotPersist to sort and stop then run the plunk again to see that persistance is not given.

It works, however, for the suggested solution. I created a stSortx directive (mostly copying stSortx) and added a //NEW comment to highlight the changes. In short, I also save the plain attribute content as a string, save it to a new property of the tablestate and compare it later to check whether the directive is currently responsible for sorting.
It does not need a function name and is simple imho.

I hope it helps.

Otherwise good work! :+1:

issue_body:
I get a RegistrationError which I do not really understand in the following way:

I have an application with the following models.py, say:

```
from django.db import models
from django.contrib.contenttypes.models import ContentType
from django.contrib.contenttypes import generic
from django.contrib import admin
import reversion

class ModelA1(models.Model):
    content_type = models.ForeignKey(ContentType)
    object_id = models.PositiveIntegerField()
    content_object = generic.GenericForeignKey('content_type', 'object_id')

class ModelB(models.Model):
    pass

class ModelC(models.Model):
    a1 = generic.GenericRelation(ModelA1, content_type_field='content_type', object_id_field='object_id')
              

class ModelBInline(generic.GenericTabularInline):
    model = ModelB

class ModelCAdmin(reversion.VersionAdmin):
    inlines = [ ModelBInline, ]

admin.site.register(ModelC, ModelCAdmin)
```

Now I create an instance c of ModelC, and one of ModelA1 pointing to c:

```
(revtest)ug@x220:~/devel/revtest/reversion_test$ python manage.py shell
Python 2.7.2+ (default, Oct  4 2011, 20:06:09) 
>>> import app1.models as m
>>> c = m.ModelC()
>>> c.save()
>>> a = m.ModelA1()
>>> a.content_object = c

Now python manage.py createinitialrevisions gives me

The problem seems to be that reversion tries to get an adapter for ModelA1. But why is it trying to follow ModelA1? What I find particularly confusing here is that the problem goes away, if I replace the line inlines = [ ModelBInline, ] in the definition of ModelCAdmin by pass.

issue_title: pyroaring doesn't adhere to python set conventions
issue_body:
Really nice package, I'm using it in (soon-to-be) production code!

The current behavior (checkout `3c3e3be`) is different from python native sets and so you can't really use pyroaring as a drop-in replacement.

intersection and union should be instance methods and not class methods:
```python
from pyroaring import BitMap
set1 = {3, 18}
set2 = {3, 27, 42}
assert (set1 & set2) == set1.intersection(set2)  # succeeds

bm1 = BitMap([3, 18])
bm2 = BitMap([3, 27, 42])
assert (bm1 & bm2) == bm1.intersection(bm2)  # fails
```
Some methods aren't implemented:
```python
assert (set1 - set2) == set1.difference(set2) #succeeds
assert (bm1 - bm2) == bm1.difference(bm2) #object has no attribute 'difference'

assert not set1.issuperset(set2) #succeeds
assert not bm1.issuperset(bm2) #object has no attribute 'issuperset'
... #and so on
```
Changing these behaviors would be a breaking change and it's understandable if you don't want to break existing code. But if there's any interest to move towards compatibility with python native sets, I'd be willing to contribute.

issue_title: File name should use forward slash even in Windows
issue_body:
I'm using `ImageField` and `django-imagekit` with django-s3-storage and I noticed that file `name` of a `ImageField/FileField` is using Windows path separators, e.g.

```
"photo_id": "family-child\\26-abu-bakar.jpg",
"photo_url": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/media.keluargasamara.com/prd/family-child/26-abu-bakar.jpg",
"thumbnail_id": "CACHE\\images\\family-child\\26-abu-bakar\\352a50472bc938d5e792c202401046be.jpg",
"thumbnail_url": "https://s3.amazonaws.com/media.keluargasamara.com/prd/CACHE/images/family-child/26-abu-bakar/352a50472bc938d5e792c202401046be.jpg",
```

Note that I recently migrated from `django-storages` due to [this bug](https://github.com/matthewwithanm/django-imagekit/issues/391#issuecomment-306754822). django-storages properly used forward slash, but it made django-imagekit unusable (For now).

I presume that this bug doesn't occur on Linux OS but the behavior should be consistent on Windows, because these paths are **S3 paths, they're not Windows paths**.

issue_title: If "~/.ssh/known_hosts" does not exist, SFTP backend fails
issue_body:
In the SFTPStorage backend, the _connect() method attempts to "automatically add host keys from the current user," by loading the "~/.ssh/known_hosts" file. However, it doesn't check if that file actually exists before trying to use it. I spent a while trying to figure out why the SFTP backend wasn't working on my own server before pinpointing this issue. This happens on line 97 of sftpstorage.py:

`self._ssh.load_host_keys(os.path.expanduser(os.path.join("~", ".ssh", "known_hosts")))`

Perhaps it would be useful to wrap that line in a try/except?

issue_title: response dict items order
issue_body:
Hi,

In some cases order of json items is very important. For example when using api of random.org. It uses a digital signature of returned values. And when you change the order, the signature verification fails. Just look at the small example.

```python
import pyjsonrpc


api_key = '123'

randomorg = pyjsonrpc.HttpClient(url='https://api.random.org/json-rpc/1/invoke')
resp = randomorg.generateSignedIntegers(api_key, 1, 1, 5)

print json.dumps(resp['random'], separators=(',', ':'))
```
result:
>{"min":1,"max":5,"completionTime":"2015-01-03 10:50:22Z","serialNumber":39,"n":1,"base":10,"hashedApiKey":"O8nkxDbtc9xQ4G3czXVKeBRutDIjTrMvXGX82Jc7++3zPPa19cphTdqzYACPlnZsbsoiiYYvFuv3FKh6siqplw==","data":[5],"method":"generateSignedIntegers","replacement":true}

And the correct implementation:
```python
import json
import collections
import requests


api_key = '123'

url = 'https://api.random.org/json-rpc/1/invoke'
headers = {'content-type': 'application/json'}

payload = {
    "method": "generateSignedIntegers",
    "params": {
        "apiKey": api_key,
        "n": 1,
        "min": 1,
        "max": 5,
    },
    "jsonrpc": "2.0",
    "id": 0,
}

resp = requests.post(
    url, data=json.dumps(payload), headers=headers).text

resp = json.loads(resp, object_pairs_hook=collections.OrderedDict)['result']

print json.dumps(resp['random'], separators=(',', ':'))
```
result:
>{"method":"generateSignedIntegers","hashedApiKey":"O8nkxDbtc9xQ4G3czXVKeBRutDIjTrMvXGX82Jc7++3zPPa19cphTdqzYACPlnZsbsoiiYYvFuv3FKh6siqplw==","n":1,"min":1,"max":5,"replacement":true,"base":10,"data":[5],"completionTime":"2015-01-03 10:53:36Z","serialNumber":40}

Sorry for my english.

issue_title: Incompatibility with django-compressor 2.1
issue_body:
*django-compressor* released a [breaking change](https://github.com/django-compressor/django-compressor/commit/c0010bf2ae4064c11cfee2387f6e06c3880fc6d3#diff-01dc27e1db84256620f15d8a69b327caL119) to their interface from
`compressor.offline.django.DjangoParser.get_nodelist(self, node, original)`
to
`compressor.offline.django.DjangoParser.get_nodelist(self, node, original, context)`

so `sass_processor.management.commands.compilescss.Command.walk_nodes()` is now broken.

I see no easy way to make this change backward compatible with `django-compressor` <= 2.0 *and* Python 2, as the introspection API to get callable's signature was also changed. I suppose, the best course of actions is to ask `django-compressor` to make this `context` argument have a default value (`None`).

(I'm ended up with hard switching to compressor 2.1, as I no longer need 2.0 compatibility)

issue_title: Few middleware problems? Django 1.11.2
issue_body:
middleware.py
```
                our_decorator = watch_login()
                watch_login_method = method_decorator(our_decorator)
```
Whats the point of this mess? Besides it throwing `TypeError: watch_login() takes exactly 1 argument (0 given)` can't it be just one line like it was before?

Like this: `watch_login_method = method_decorator(watch_login)`

Also for django-defender to work with the new django **MIDDLEWARE** I need to do the following to the middleware:
```
try:
    from django.utils.deprecation import MiddlewareMixin as MIDDLEWARE_BASE_CLASS
except ImportError:
    MIDDLEWARE_BASE_CLASS = object
```

issue_title: Translated models doesn't return flat annotated values_lists
issue_body:
Translated models returns list of tuples when queryset is build as `values_list(...,  flat=True).annotate(...)`. Expected behaviour is to return list of values regardless of the order of `values_list` and `annotate` calls.

Found using django-modeltranslation 0.11 and django 1.7 and 1.8

##### Example #####
```python
# Untranslated model
>>> Foo.objects.values_list('year', flat=True).annotate(Count('year'))
[1991, 1992, 1993, 1994, ...]
# Translated model
>>> Bar.objects.values_list('year', flat=True).annotate(Count('year')) # Unexpected result
[(1991, 2), (1992, 1), (1993, 5), (1994, 7), ...]
# Same output is given when `flat` is not used
>>> Bar.objects.values_list('year').annotate(Count('year'))
[(1991, 2), (1992, 1), (1993, 5), (1994, 7), ...]
```

_Edit: I removed the cases `annotate(...).values_list(...)`, because it performs different queries, both in django and modeltranslation._

issue_title: Why LGPL?
issue_body:
https://github.com/koalalorenzo/python-digitalocean/issues/33

LGPL wasn't written with scripting languages in mind. It's still a huge license that can create derivatives. It hasn't been tried in court in any jurisdiction I operate in to my knowledge (USA + IL). It's ambiguous on what creates a derivative. I don't want to risk creating one or being accused of doing so just to use a python library for digital ocean.

Since this is the most popular package for digital ocean and python, would you consider switching it to MIT/BSD/ISC/Apache2, etc?

For the moment, does anyone know any alternatives without viral clauses?

issue_title: Equal phonenumbers not equal
issue_body:
When you create two records with phonenumberfield, one with '+44 113 8921113' and other with '+441138921113' value and than compare them, they are not equal (```comp_before``` is ```False```).

```python
class MandatoryPhoneNumber(models.Model):
    phone_number = PhoneNumberField()

first = MandatoryPhoneNumber.objects.create(phone_number='+44 113 8921113')
second = MandatoryPhoneNumber.objects.create(phone_number='+441138921113')

comp_before = first.phone_number == second.phone_number

first.save()
second.save()

first_db = MandatoryPhoneNumber.objects.get(id=first.id)
second_db = MandatoryPhoneNumber.objects.get(id=second.id)

comp_after = first_db.phone_number == second_db.phone_number
```

This is because parent class for ```PhoneNumberField``` compares in ```__eq__``` function every attribute of phonenumber and for the '+44 113 8921113' and '+441138921113' differs ```raw_input``` attribute.

But when you save these two records to database and than read them from database, these two records are equal (```comp_after``` is ```True```), because we store phone number in database as string with constant format and initialize from database provided "normalized" string format.

And this is inconsistency - ```comp_before != comp_after```. It must be ```comp_before == comp_after```

issue_title: Run as Admin for Sikuli to Interact with Microsoft Management Console. Please add to Release Note/Readme.
issue_body:
I would like to add a note that through my recent testing, that Microsoft Management Console (MMC) will not let SikuliX perform any actions on it, unless SikuliX itself is started as an administrator. If you are using SikuliX only, it is easy, just start SikuliX IDE or run the SikuliX script in a console that was started as an administrator (ie: right click on cmd/console and select "run as administrator"). However, if you are using the SikuliLibrary and Robot framework, SikuliLibrary itself also needs to be started from the cmd window that was "run as administrator". Otherwise, you will get a lot of errors like:

```
[log] CLICK on L(1061,118)@S(0)[0,0 1920x1080]
[error] RobotDesktop: checkMousePosition: should be L(1061,118)@S(0)[0,0 1920x1080]
but after move is L(137,215)@S(0)[0,0 1920x1080]
Possible cause in case you did not touch the mouse while script was running:
 Mouse actions are blocked generally or by the frontmost application.
You might try to run the SikuliX stuff as admin.
```

Besides the above log error, the symptom is that the mouse itself does not move and will never interact with the MMC UI.

I have also set my UAC to the lowest level (not to notify the user). This does not mean that UAC is turned off, just that it does not have any unnecessary popup when your tests are being run (or you will have to take care of them in your test scripts).

It would help to add these observations to the readme/release notes, maybe under a Microsoft Windows section.

issue_title: Design Doc: HTTP Healthcheck on Deploy
issue_body:
refs the new proposal in #4019. All input/feedback on the doc is welcome.
refs #3813

During a typical deployment of an application release, the controller performs a status check from Fleet after it has been deployed to ensure that it is running. However, Fleet is a glorified systemd wrapper, which means that it only checks systemd if the process is running (in this case, `docker run`). If a container's web process takes some time to boot, this may cause a false positive status check from the user's perspective. This proposal introduces a new concept into Deis known as a health check, which will make the controller perform an HTTP health check to ensure the container is running before recycling the old release.

Goals
-----

The main goals of this proposal is to

 - ensure a zero-downtime deployment between deployments of new releases
 - allow the user to configure the URL path and the expected status code on that endpoint

Note that the intention of this document is to **not** introduce changes to deis-publisher to perform HTTP health checks while the application is running. We [found issues with this approach](https://github.com/deis/deis/pull/3982#issuecomment-120112608) that conflicted with the expected workflow. Since the main goal of this design is to support a zero-downtime approach to deployments, this has been scoped out to keep changes at a minimum for review and tests. A follow-up doc may be necessary to tackle #3813's third point.

Code Changes
------------

In order for this change to occur, there needs to be a way for users to configure both the expected health check URL as well as the expected status code. Since the `Config` object currently exists and the health checks are considered part of an application's configuration, the code changes in the controller is fairly minimal:

 - `deis config:set HEALTHCHECK_URL=/` will configure a health check URL for the application
 - by default, the expected status code from the health check is 200, though users can change this
   with `deis config:set HEALTHCHECK_STATUS_CODE=999`.

Once `HEALTHCHECK_URL` is set, the controller will do the typical application release workflow. However, once the controller is notified by Fleet that the new version of the application is "up", the controller will now continuously check the application's health by making requests against the publicly available URL, which means going through the router. This health check will continuously perform an HTTP request every second for 60 seconds. If the health check passes, the rest of the deployment workflow will continue, recycling old containers and returning a successful response to the client. However, if at any time we receive a request that does not match the expected status code, then the controller will respond with an appropriate error and roll back to the old version of the application.

Testing
-------

In order for this proposal to work, we need to ensure a zero-downtime deployment, as well as proper error codes to be returned when an application fails to respond in time or with an incorrect status code. Since the controller has a great suite for unit tests, most of the workflow for the client will be covered by unit tests to ensure that the proper errors are propagated when certain cases occur. However, the zero-downtime approach will be covered by integration tests. How this will be accomplished is by invoking a goroutine that will continuously ping the app URL. If a 404 response shows up (due to #3774), then the deployment failed as a zero-downtime deployment.

issue_body:
Django provides [Form fields](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.9/ref/forms/fields/) that can be used directly in `Form` objects.

Does `django-enumfields` provide an `EnumField` that can be used directly in a form like:

```
from django import forms

class MyForm(forms.Form):
    my_enum = enumfields.  # ... ?
```

I can get it to work if I put an `enumfields.EnumIntegerField` into a `Model` and then use a `ModelForm`, but if I just want to create a `Form` without a `Model` I don't know how to do this.

I'm happy to submit a PR with an example if someone could teach me how to do this. :)

issue_title: Layer options defaults not set if options section missing
issue_body: If no layer's `layer.yaml` includes an options section, no options end up in the rendered `layer.yaml` even if defaults are set.  Even having an empty options section (i.e., `options: {}`) is sufficient to get the options with defaults to render.

issue_title: Definitions should gracefully accept unknown params
issue_body: From discussions in Montreal, it seems that new fields in the Juju schema definitions are expected to be handled gracefully even without a bump to the facade version.  That is, facade versions will only be bumped if a field is removed or changed.

issue_title: Indeterminate order generates unneccessary diffs in Pipfile.lock when upgrading.
issue_body:
My `Pipfile.lock` contains many lines of the following form:

    "markers": "python_version != '3.2.*' and python_version != '3.0.*' and python_version != '3.1.*' and python_version >= '2.7' and python_version != '3.3.*'",

and after an unrelated change, this line is changed into

    "markers": "python_version != '3.0.*' and python_version != '3.2.*' and python_version != '3.3.*' and python_version != '3.1.*' and python_version >= '2.7'",

i.e. the same content but different order. It would be great to keep the order deterministic in order to keep the diffs small when updating.

issue_title: The `az acr login` command is a bit unfriendly
issue_body:
With the Azure Container Registry we need to use `az acr login`.

However, it is a bit unfiendly because:

1. Users need to know in which subscription the registry is.
2. Users need to issue `az account set` command first. This itself is a dangerous one.

It would be lovely to remove the need to know the sub for ACR altogeher.

If that is not possible at all, it would make things easier if we could have `--subscription` arg in `az acr login`. 

See also https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli/issues/5618, which would help if implemented.

PS. It would be even nicer if we could use `docker login` with user credentials directly without the need to have `azure CLI`, because TBH it's a bit heavy-handed option (but I realise it's not azure cli issue per se).

issue_title: az vm access set-linux-user --password makes insecure changes the SSHD configuration
issue_body:
CLI Version: 0.1.0b9
OS Type: Mac
Installation via:  pip

Environment: AzureCloud

Description:
Using `az access set-linux-user --password`  to reset a Linux users password, makes insecure changes to the SSH servers configuration `/etc/ssh/sshd_config` and allows password logins by changing the value of `PasswordAuthentication` to `yes`.

Steps to reproduce:
1) deploy Linux VM using SSH keys for authentication `--ssh-key-value ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub \` with a user called `ahmet`
2) login to the Linux VM and verify the setting for `PasswordAuthentication` in `/etc/ssh/sshd_config` is `no`
3) from the `azure-cli` reset user Ahmets password - `az vm access set-linux-user -u ahmet -p bmwbmwbmw`
4) login to the Linux VM and verify the settings for `PasswordAuthentication` in `/etc/ssh/sshd_config` is *now* `yes`


Expected Behavior:

Resetting a users password with vm access-extensions should _only_ change that users password.  

`az vm access` would not make any system wide security changes

Current Behavior:

Using `az vm access set-linux-user` to make changes to a Linux user, makes an insecure change on the entire Linux VM by opening up SSH to password brute forcing attempts by allowing passwords for SSH logins.  All unknown to the admin of the Linux VM.

Error stack trace:
no error logs

node-cli bug filed: https://github.com/Azure/azure-xplat-cli/issues/3335

issue_title: Feature request: Allow edit of AD application manifest
issue_body:
Currently there is no way to update the manifest of an Azure AD application programatically without using the Windows PowerShell.

Even though I am only interested in updating the `groupMembershipClaims` to `All`, a more generic update would benefit other users as well.

## Environment

My CLI version: azure-cli (2.0.30)

issue_title: az webapp config appsettings set should not print out all settings
issue_body:
### Description
The  `az webapp config appsettings set` command currently prints out all settings for the webapp, which is unexpected and has an unintended consequence of printing sensitive information to the console. For example, if you run:
`az webapp config container set --name somename --docker-custom-image-name ${DOCKER_REGISTRY}/someimage--docker-registry    -server-url https://${DOCKER_REGISTRY} --docker-registry-server-password ${DOCKER_PASSWORD} --docker-registry-server-user ${DOCKER_USER}`

Then the registry credentials are automatically set in appsettings. If someone later runs:
`az webapp config appsettings set --settings PORT=8080 --name somename`

Then the username and password for the registry get printed to the screen.

As a general rule, set commands shouldn't print out state information.

issue_title: Add unqouted/raw strings to the JMESPath query
issue_body:
**Problem**
I've been using the `--query` parameter recently to automatically retrieve access keys and other information for my cache instances. I find it better then using a package like jq or python as it requires less dependencies. I load those into a variable to easily pass to other commands e.g. redis-cli.
```bash
adam@adam-dev:~$ key=$(az redis list-keys --resource-group $rg --name $name --query primaryKey)
adam@adam-dev:~$ echo $key
"fakeAcessKey123"
adam@adam-dev:~$ 
```
Unfortunately, the query parameter prints quoted strings and doesn't have an option for raw strings (no quotes).

**Possible solution**
JMESPath's CLI supports [unquoted output](https://github.com/jmespath/jp#unquoted-output). I'd like to see this supported in the Azure CLI's query parameter.

**Alternatives attempted**
I've accomplished the same effect using other dependencies to format the JSON like query+sed, however this is tied to unix and is harder to mentally parse.
```bash
adam@adam-dev:~$ az redis list-keys --resource-group $rg --name $name --query primaryKey | sed -e 's/^"//' -e 's/"$//'
fakeAccessKey123
adam@adam-dev:~$ 
```

Also accomplished with Python, a more cross-platform friendly version, but requires the python CLI to be installed.
```bash
adam@adam-dev:~$ az redis list-keys --resource-group $rg --name $name | python3 -c "import sys, json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['primaryKey'])"
fakeAccessKey123
adam@adam-dev:~$ 
```

I also discovered where outputting as tsv strips the quotes away. This feels unintentional, hacky, and has a potential to change in future versions.
```bash
adam@adam-dev:~$ az redis list-keys --resource-group $rg --name $name --query primaryKey --output tsv
fakeAccessKey123
adam@adam-dev:~$ 
```
Additionally, it does not work with multilevel data such as querying for cache name and sku. The first command doesn't print anything related to sku.
```bash
adam@adam-dev:~$ az redis list --query "[].{sku: sku, name: name}" --output tsv
        adam-cache-0
        adam-cache-1
        adam-cache-2
adam@adam-dev:~$ az redis list --query "[].{sku: sku, name: name}"
[
  {
    "name": "adam-cache-0",
    "sku": {
      "capacity": 1,
      "family": "C",
      "name": "Standard"
    }
  },
  {
    "name": "adam-cache-1",
    "sku": {
      "capacity": 2,
      "family": "C",
      "name": "Basic"
    }
  },
  {
    "name": "adam-cache-2",
    "sku": {
      "capacity": 1,
      "family": "P",
      "name": "Premium"
    }
  }
]
adam@adam-dev:~$ 
```

issue_title: Better name in makefile
issue_body:
Follow up #1236

You might actually want to use the [standard names](https://www.gnu.org/prep/standards/html_node/Directory-Variables.html) (i.e. lowercase `prefix`, `*dir`), something like this:

```Makefile
# Common prefix for installation directories.
# NOTE: This directory must exist when you start the install.
prefix = /usr/local
datarootdir = $(prefix)/share
datadir = $(datarootdir)
exec_prefix = $(prefix)
bindir = $(exec_prefix)/bin
localedir = $(datarootdir)/locale
gsettingsschemadir=$(datarootdir)/share/glib-2.0/schemas
…
```

I also think `INSTALL_ROOT` is commonly called [`DESTDIR`](https://www.gnu.org/prep/standards/html_node/DESTDIR.html).

cc @aichingm

issue_title: Replace libkeybinder by a GAction under Wayland
issue_body:
See [FeatHub vote here](https://feathub.com/Guake/guake/+29).

Under wayland, libkeybinder requires to force use of XWayland (X11 backend). This does not seem to do the job since some complain Guake does not show up if the current focussed application if a GTK3 application.

It seems the cleaner solution would be to develop a [GAction](https://developer.gnome.org/gio/stable/GAction.html) to replace the use of libkeybinder under Gnome/Wayland. This would not completely replace libkeybinder, since other DE (such as XFCE or KDE) won't see the "GAction". Maybe a plugin system should be developed to easily extend the global hotkey support in the future (`plugins/hotkeys/{gnome.py,kde.py,xfce.py,libkeybinder.py...}`).

issue_title: Conda (4.4): 'No such file or directory' in a new environment using non-shell process
issue_body:
<!--
Hi!

This is an issue tracker for conda -- the package manager.  File feature requests
for conda here, as well as bug reports about something conda has messed up.

If your issue is a bug report or feature request for:
  * a specific conda package from Anaconda ('defaults' channel):
      ==> file at https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues
  * a specific conda package from conda-forge:
      ==> file at the corresponding feedstock under https://github.com/conda-forge
  * repo.continuum.io access and service:
      ==> file at https://github.com/ContinuumIO/anaconda-issues
  * anaconda.org access and service:
      ==> file at https://anaconda.org/contact/report
  * commands under 'conda build':
      ==> file at https://github.com/conda/conda-build
  * commands under 'conda env':
      ==> please file it here!
  * all other conda commands that start with 'conda':
      ==> please file it here!

-->

**I'm submitting a...** (I don't know whether it is a bug or feature)
  - [ X] bug report
  - [ X] feature request


## Current Behavior
<!-- What actually happens?
     If you want to include console output, please use "Steps to Reproduce" below. -->


### Steps to Reproduce
<!-- If the current behavior is a bug, please provide specific, minimal steps to independently reproduce.
     Include the exact conda commands that reproduce the issue and their output between the ticks below. -->
Create an environment, test, using Conda 4.4, open a new tab and directly activate that environment. Now try to invoke conda commands using any non shell program (lua/python/java/....) it will error out saying `conda: command not found` or `No such file or directory` since they can't `execve()` that.

## Expected Behavior
<!-- What do you think should happen? -->
The program invoking conda should not error out, or should have a way to figure out where the conda  resides, so that it can execve the full `/path/to/conda`. Currently the only thing close to this is `$_CONDA_EXE`, but it is not documented, and also not exported, so not available to child processes and no guarantee whether it will be available in future releases.

The current work around is to first activate the base environment and then activate the non-base environment in the same shell session, which for whatever reason, retains the root environment's bin directory in the PATH.

issue_title: conda --flle option improvements
issue_body:
1. Allow shorthand `-f` option like pip
2. Provide a method to split a pip requirements.txt into two files: `conda-requirements.txt` and `pip-requirements.txt`
3. Conda should print **all** package not found messages at once. for large `requirements.txt`  files it gets painful to iterate over:
   
   ```

issue_title: Activate script should disable user site-packages directory
issue_body: The current `activate` scripts are error-prone in that they allow Python to see non-environment-installed packages. They should really set `PYTHONNOUSERSITE`.

For my Message handler need quit a long time to process, I enable the message asynchronously as follows:
```
def handler(message):
    message.enable_async()
    # process it for a long time
    deal_with_task()
    message.finish()
```

I am runing `Arch Linux`
``` sh
$ uname -a
Linux tardis 3.18.6-1-ARCH #1 SMP PREEMPT Sat Feb 7 08:44:05 CET 2015 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

I followed these [installation instructions][1]. Specifically, my actions were:

``` sh
$ git clone https://github.com/pgmpy/pgmpy
$ cd pgmpy
$ virtualenv --system-site-packages vipgmpy
Using base prefix '/usr'
New python executable in vipgmpy/bin/python3
Also creating executable in vipgmpy/bin/python
Installing setuptools, pip...done.
$ source ./vipgmpy/bin/activate
$ python setup.py install
```
At this point, the `setup.py` finds all the required packages except one: `numpy`. That is because in `Arch Linux` the version of this package compiled with `openblas` support is called `numpy-openblas`. So, `setup.py` installs the vanilla `numpy` into the `vipgmpy` virtual environment. To be precise, here is the [verbose list of my dependencies][2].

At this point everything is fine. I fire up `ipython`, issue `import pgmp`. It succeeds. So I decide to run the tests. From the source directory, I run `nosetests` and get a huge sheet of [failed tests][3].

What am I doing wrong?

issue_title: user.get_roles does not include local roles inherited from groups when using "inherit=False"
issue_body:
As far as I know the `inherit=False` in `user.get_roles(user=foo, obj=bar, inherit=False)` is meant to ignore global roles. What it also does is ignore roles gotten from local roles of groups on the same object. That is inconsistent with `user.get_roles(user=foo, obj=bar, inherit=True)` which returns global and local roles owned by the user and local and gloabl roles inherited from groups the user is in.
The feature was added by @cillianderoiste in 654f749ff7a79eb7a21915b4a1b88f4870c51cd7.

issue_title: physicaliam returns np.array for pd.Series input
issue_body:
Like most of our functions, physicaliam is supposed to return an output of the same type as the input (Series in, Series out). The function works with only numpy arrays internally, so it has a couple of lines to make the output into a Series, if necessary:

```python
if isinstance(aoi, pd.Series):
    iam = pd.Series(iam, index=aoi.index)
```

where ``aoi`` is supposed to be the unmodified input to the function.

PR #338 added this line:

```python
aoi = np.where(aoi == 0, zeroang, aoi)
```

but, ``np.where`` always returns an array, so the ``ifinstance`` always returns ``False``.

The long term solution is a smarter way to deal with in/out types. A short term solution is to stash a reference to the input object before reassigning the ``aoi`` object reference: 

```python
aoi_input = aoi
aoi = np.where(aoi == 0, zeroang, aoi)
# unmodified function
if isinstance(aoi_input, pd.Series):
    iam = pd.Series(iam, index= aoi_input.index)
```

issue_title: ENH: rename singlediode_methods.py to singlediode.py and discuss pvsystem.singlediode refactor
issue_body:
**Rename module name `singlediode_methods` and refactor `pvsystem.singlediode**
* `singlediode_methods` is a long name
* the suffix "methods" is ambiguous and not descriptive
* the other modules have meaningful names that correspond loosely to [the modeling steps described at Sandia PVPMC](https://pvpmc.sandia.gov/modeling-steps/)
* there needs to be a discussion regarding possibly refactoring `pvsystem.singlediode`
    - having a module shadow a method from a _different_ module might be confusing or maybe it's not a problem?
    - has there already been discussion on breaking `singlediode` up, refactoring, or renaming it?

**rename module `singlediode_methods.py` to singlediode.py and consider refactoring `pvsystem.singlediode`**
1. rename the module from `singlediode_methods.py` to just `singlediode.py`
2. change all references in the code where appropriate
3. change all references in the comments and documentation where appropriate
4. start a discussion on the repercussions to the `singlediode()` methods in `pvsystem.py`

**alternatives**
* rename the module something else like `dc_energy.py`, `pvmodules.py`, or `pvcells.py`
* do nothing
* rename the module to `singlediode.py` and leave `pvsystem.singlediode()` alone
* rename the module to `singlediode.py` and mode `pvsystem.singlediode()` to `singlediode.singlediode()` which is a more established paradigm, right?

**Additional context**
* this is a follow on to #504
* comments in #497
* also related to #408, #409, and #410

issue_title: cookbook should link to textbook fifth web edition, not 4th
Git protocols
issue_body:
I have a few suggestions for good git protocols, especially as our project may be growing to include more contributors.  

1. Commits should have descriptive names that briefly explain what was done

2. Commit names like “Fixes #100” should have additional context so that they make sense when you aren’t looking at the git log through Github.

3. Commits should deal with one concern at a time — spanning multiple files is fine, but commits with many unrelated changes are confusing and should be broken up.  This makes it easier for someone else to understand what happened, and if something broke in a particular commit it is easier to track it down if the changes are more isolated.  

Anybody have others?  I think it will be helpful for us to try to be more strict on these things :)  I am happy to incorporate these items into CONTRIBUTING.

issue_title: [MKLDNN Bug] MKLDNN eats lots of memory and then crash down.
issue_body:
MKLDNN eats lots of memory and crash down with **big** input image. You can try [a project of mine](https://github.com/chinakook/hr101_mxnet) to issue that. The ordinary MKL can run it successfully in 8GB memory, but MKLDNN eat all 64GB of my PC.
**Big** input data are very common used in little object detection in a big scene and regular scene segmentation.

issue_title: feature request: tensor contraction
issue_body:
Dear all, 

is there a (simple) way to do tensor contraction with ```NDArray```? Say I have a tensor T^{ij}_{kl} (i.e. a 4D NDArray with dim(j) = dim(k) ), I want to perform contraction over the j,k indices. For example, if ```dim(j) == dim(k) = 5``` I need to perform the operation: 
```
Result^i_k = \sum_{j=0}^{4} T^{ij}_{jk}
```
this is different than writing ```nd.sum(T,axis=[2,3])``` which sums also for indices ```j != k ```.  

As another example, for a 2D NDArray
```Python
data = nd.ones(shape = [5,5])

temp = 0.0
for i in range(5):
    temp += data[i,i]
```
here the variable ```temp``` (contraction) has the value 5 (correct), while ```nd.sum(data,axis=[0,1])``` results 25 (not contraction). 

If such a functionality is not yet implemented (from what I've seen it doesn't exist), could you please consider adding it? 
Thank you very much.

I'm trying to use minpy which requires mxnet in anaconda's jupyter notebook. minpy can be installed using anaconda's pip successfully.

However there is an issue on installing mxnet. I noticed using anaconda's "pip install mxnet" does not work (opencv not found, although opencv.pc clearly exists and the path is correctly set). So I wonder how can I install mxnet into anaconda's python, instead of system's default python by changing some settings? I figured out I can disable using opencv by changing config.mk file. I used a similar way to get both minpy and mxnet work on system's default python.

Thank you.

issue_title: rec generated by im2rec.py has same value for different key in idx
issue_body:
I generate a large rec file (over 1M files) using im2rec with MXIndexedRecordIO and observe that in data.idx file, after specific lines, the first number in each line keeps increasing while the second one keeps unchanged, like this:

> 32989	18446744073709551615
> 32990	18446744073709551615
> 32991	18446744073709551615
> 32992	18446744073709551615
> 32993	18446744073709551615
> 32994	18446744073709551615
> 32995	18446744073709551615
> 32997	18446744073709551615
> 32998	18446744073709551615


From this line https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/22b2897d23de44c696c7776a7a2ba1879be5eb2e/python/mxnet/recordio.py#L226, it seems that the second number determines the position for a specific key. So different key should have a different position. I have tried read through the full rec file and found that the last few items are not the same and their position should be different. So what does the second column in idx file mean and is it normal to have same values in second columns? Is there any documentation? Thanks.

issue_title: Implementing New Operators
issue_body:
I would like to implement a new operator with MXNet, and the operator is already implemented with Caffe. What is the easiest way to port my Caffe implementation to MXNet?

The comment https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/issues/7652#issuecomment-325894475 says that it is easier to implement complex operators using the legacy interface. But will the **legacy interface** be unsupported in the future? If it is OK to use the legacy interface, can someone provide links to some **comprehensive tutorials or examples**?

-------

To use the new nnvm interface for defining operators, I read this document: [A Beginner's Guide to Implementing Operators in MXNet Backend](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/docs/how_to/add_op_in_backend.md). The step-by-step explanations are clear and I have understood the general pipeline of defining a new operator, but there still seems to be some obstacles in the way.

1. How can I define **_variables storing intermediate results_** which can be used in both forward and backward computations? These variables will act like class member variables (assuming the operator is defined as a class). This is similar to the question in the comment https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/issues/7652#issuecomment-338910634.
2. How can I define a _**operator with learnable parameters**_ (e.g. convolution operator)? The current implementations of operators such as convolution seem to use a different (and legacy) interface. And the [tensor examples](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/tree/master/src/operator/tensor) are all parameter-free operators like dot product.
3. Where can I find _**explanations of the various macros**_ to be used in operator definitions? The many macros are very confusing and it is hard for me to determine when I should use what and how. It would be very helpful if some templates for typical usages can be provided and explained.
4. How can I **_correctly reference the headers_** in [src/operator](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/tree/master/src/operator), if I put my operator definition files in an independent directory outside MXNet? Many source files simply use relative paths, e.g. `#include #include "../mxnet_op.h"` and `#include "../mshadow_op.h"`, which are not suitable if a file is located outside MXNet directories.
5. How can I **_specify multiple paths for the_** `EXTRA_OPERATORS` in  [config.mk](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/make/config.mk)? After checking the [Makefile](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/Makefile) and [CMakeLists.txt](https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/CMakeLists.txt), I find that only one path is supported (Please correct me if I am wrong).

issue_title: ResNet-50 is slower on Volta since #8302
issue_body:
## Description
I ran the Minimum reproducible example with the setup below at two different version (before and after #8302): 
Here are the results:
d03182f4fc084741ea185aac2eb02d0e7b4151f9 (before #8302):
    - real data: 5644 samples / s
    - synthetic data: 5971 samples / s
c3e3a832bfeceff070ba263aa8a4489ca27f452e (after #8302):
    - real data: 5461 samples / s
    - synthetic data: 5740 samples / s
Latest:
    - real data: 5425 samples / s
    - synthetic data: 5817 samples / s

@ptrendx @DickJC123 @mkolod 


## Environment info (Required)
CPUs: Intel Xeon E5-2698 v4 (x2)
GPUs: Nvidia V100 (x8)


## Build info (Required if built from source)
From the default config.mk (in make/config.mk) added:
```USE_CUDA=1
USE_CUDNN=1
CUDA_ARCH := -gencode arch=compute_52,code=sm_52 -gencode arch=compute_60,code=sm_60 -gencode arch=compute_61,code=sm_61 -gencode arch=compute_70,code=sm_70 -gencode arch=compute_70,code=compute_70
USE_CUDA_PATH=/usr/local/cuda
USE_LIBJPEG_TURBO=1
USE_LIBJPEG_TURBO_PATH=/usr
USE_NCCL=1```


## Minimum reproducible example
python /mxnet/example/image-classification/train_imagenet.py --benchmark 0 --gpu 0,1,2,3,4,5,6,7 --batch-size 1024 --num-epochs 1 --data-train /data/imagenet/train-480-val-256-recordio/train.rec --data-train-idx /data/imagenet/train-480-val-256-recordio/train.idx --data-val /data/imagenet/train-480-val-256-recordio/val.rec --disp-batches 100 --network resnet-v1 --num-layers 50 --data-nthreads 40 --min-random-scale 0.533 --max-random-shear-ratio 0 --max-random-rotate-angle 0 --max-random-h 0 --max-random-l 0 --max-random-s 0 --dtype float16 --kv-store device

issue_title: Does mxnet support  bounded-daly consistency model now?
issue_body:
## Description
I read the paper of MXNet 

> MXNet: A Flexible and Efficient Machine Learning Library for Heterogeneous Distributed Systems
in part 2.3,it says  "Currently, we support the sequential and eventual consistency." however ,th kv-store implemetion in parameter support bounded-delay consistency model.

I want to know  whether  mxnet support  bounded-daly consistency model now?
And what method the distributred traning process deploy? BSP, SSP or other ?

issue_title: assimp-config.cmake sets incorrect ASSIMP_ROOT_DIR when library path is deeply nested.
issue_body:
This may or may not prove related to #1912 but when ASSIMP_LIB_INSTALL_DIR contains more than one directory level the logic below isn't sufficient to find the root directory producing a broken CMake configuration.

https://github.com/assimp/assimp/blob/620182770da9f851a44040af69cb20ba6c3f6106/assimp-config.cmake.in#L13-L16

This affects the libassimp-dev package on Debian Stretch, Buster, and Sid and Ubuntu Artful and Bionic. Verifiable on these platforms with the following example CMakeLists.txt:
```cmake
cmake_minimum_required(VERSION 3.0)
project(asitest)

find_package(assimp)

message(STATUS "ASSIMP_ROOT_DIR: ${ASSIMP_ROOT_DIR}")
message(STATUS "ASSIMP_LIBRARY_DIRS: ${ASSIMP_LIBRARY_DIRS}")
message(STATUS "ASSIMP_INCLUDE_DIRS: ${ASSIMP_INCLUDE_DIRS}")
```

Selected output from above CMakeLists.txt:
```
...
-- ASSIMP_ROOT_DIR: /usr/lib
-- ASSIMP_LIBRARY_DIRS: /usr/lib/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu
-- ASSIMP_INCLUDE_DIRS: /usr/lib/include
...
```

issue_title: Reduce load time when loading the edit section for sites with large hierarchy
issue_body: When running worth2 locally, the "view section" view loads in 500ms, while the "edit section" view loads in 2800ms. django-debug-toolbar reports the SQL queries only took 100ms. @sdreher says this due to the checks for locking / gating. It would be good to find a way to reduce the load time here.

issue_title: Allow docker_run to return exit code rather than raising exception
issue_body: The developer may actually want to get hold of the docker exit code. I am thinking of the case where you have a couple of task chained together. If the docker running fails then the return code might be used by the next task to determine what to do next. In this case you just want to return the exit code from the task rather than raise an exception. Now in this case would you mark the task's state as error? I could argue either way. We could control this behavior by passing in another parameter, something like raise_on_error, defaulting to True.

issue_title: Pulumi fails to read input from the console on Windows when run in minTTY
issue_body:
When running Pulumi on windows inside the MinGW Shell (when it is configured to use minTTY) any of our reads we do from standard in fail with an error that the handle is invalid.  This means that any prompt the CLI tries to do fails (for example prompting for an access toen when logging in, selecting a template during `pulumi new`, entering a secret on the command line).

The problem is almost certainly related to us using Windows APIs (via third party golang packages) on handles that aren't actual windows console handles.

This problem does not reproduce in either powershell or cmd.  In ConEMU, it does reproduce when you create a new bash shell, but if you are running cmd.exe, it does not.

issue_title: Logs command spams API calls to get stack and export stack
issue_body:
It's expected that running `pulumi logs` would need to gather the stack's state and checkpoint file in order to know what resources to query. However, as the command is written today, it pulls the stack's state and checkpoint file every second along with its checking of logs which seems unnecessary.

Do we expect `pulumi logs` to pickup stack resource changes during the lifetime of the command's output? Wouldn't it be more reasonable to just fetch the stack's checkpoint once and just query logs for that set of resources until the command terminates?

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/4029847/41784990-1e130046-75f6-11e8-9033-035f46c0725c.png)

issue_title: def_buffer doesn't accept pointer-to-member-function
GCC 7.1 (mingw-w64) has broken -flto and hypot
issue_body:
For future:
GCC 7.1 (mingw-w64)
Just examine problem while compiling tests with unreferenced symbols on `Release` and `MinSizeRel` . 
`-flto` have mystic conflicts with -O3 or -Os 
Temp solution while gcc not resolve problem: 
Disable -flto flag like this:
`cmake ..  -G"Ninja" -DPYBIND11_LTO_CXX_FLAGS=""`

mingw-w64 headers have conflict with python headers: 
`error: '::hypot' has not been declared`
we can solve this with redefine `-D_hypot=hypot`
`cmake ..  -G"Ninja" -DPYBIND11_LTO_CXX_FLAGS="" -DCMAKE_CXX_FLAGS="-D_hypot=hypot"`

issue_title: Providing a unicode string to dmatrices results in a misleading error message
issue_body:
Doing this works fine:
foo = pd.DataFrame(dict(a=[1,2.0], b=[4,5.0]))
patsy.dmatrices('a ~ b', foo)

However, if the formula is given by a unicode string:
patsy.dmatrices(u'a ~ b', foo)

An exception is raised:
ValueError: design matrix must be real-valued floating point

The exception is very misleading about the nature of the problem.  I thought an inf or nan had crept into the dataframe, but no bad values were there.

One possible fix is to send the first argument of dmatrices through the str() function:
patsy.dmatrices(str(u'a ~ b'), foo)

Another possible fix is to raise print a different error message so that the user has some clear idea of the cause of the problem. 

I'm using patsy version 0.3.0

Thanks!

issue_title: N3 literal parsing failes on \t
issue_body:
hannes.g...@gmail.com, 2008-01-23T10:32:30.000Z 
When I try to parse an N3, I get an error when a literal contains a \t. But
\t is defined to be in N3 strings.

I suspect the failure is in n3proc's unquote:

>>> from rdflib.syntax.parsers.n3p.n3proc import unquote
>>> unquote("\t")
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "", line 1, in 
  File "build/bdist.linux-i686/egg/rdflib/syntax/parsers/n3p/n3proc.py",
line 85, in unquote
rdflib.syntax.parsers.n3p.n3proc.ParseError: Illegal literal character: '\t'

Comment 1 by eike...@gmail.com
This is actually correct, I believe, since "\t" should be escaped as r"\t". For example unquote(r"\t") will give you 
"\t" -- and unquote("\t") (AKA an actual tab character) will give the above error.

In looking into this issue I did notice Literal's n3 method is not correctly escaping "\t". Literal("\t").n3() should be 
"\\t" which is the same as r"\t".


Comment 2 by eike...@gmail.com



Comment 3 by gromgull
Eikeon is right - this seem to work fine

{{{
g=rdflib.graph.Graph()
g.parse(StringIO.StringIO(r'  "Gunnar\tlikes chese". '),
format="n3")
print g.serialize()


  
    <_4:name>Gunnar	likes chese
  

}}}

issue_title: MessageTooLarge uncaught failure using rdkafka
issue_body:
Note that I am using SNAPPY compression on the producer.

Has anyone attempted to send a 2mb+ message to kafka with rdkafka turned on? I noticed that it puts an exception in the delivery report queue that states that the message is too large, but doesnt throw an exception at all. It just skips sending that message

With rdkafka turned off it posts to kafka just fine, which makes me think that rdkafka is either not compressing the message OR its not using the "compressed size" of the message to compare to kafka's message.max.bytes setting. Which is currently at the default of 1000000.

So what i think may be happening is without rdfkafka the pykafka library is compressing the 2mb message below the default setting and not throwing an exception, while the rdkafka producer is NOT compressing the message prior to checking that value.

issue_title: Timeouts for post_to_esp()
issue_body:
We recently had networking troubles which lead to certain (Sendgrid) SSL handshakes stalling forever. This lead all our mail sending workers to eventually stall and lock up all background tasks.

Usually, for other HTTPS requests, we add a (generous) timeout (see http://docs.python-requests.org/en/master/user/quickstart/#timeouts). The timeout is chosen such that it only triggers in those rare, extreme circumstances. We mostly use 30 seconds, but Stripe, as another example, uses even 80 seconds (https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python/blob/f948b8b95b6df5b57c7444a05d6c83c8c5e6a0ac/stripe/http_client.py#L97).

I think anymail should have a default timeout, too. Doesn't even have to be configurable, and the exception could be passed straight to user code.
